channel.title: Lambda Expression on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts
channel.description: Recent content in Lambda Expression on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts

item.title: Let's create coffee with a decorator pattern with the help of lambda expression
item.description: java.util.function.Function<T, R> is a functional interface. It takes a type T and returns type R. We mostly use it to transform a type into a different one. But it doesn’t end there. We can use this into a lambda expression to change a value as well. For example- Function<Integer, Integer> doubleIt = a -> a * 2; Over here, we are receiving and in integer but returning after doubling it. Thus, we can pass this function as an argument of a method as well.

item.title: ArrayList ও LinkedList মধ্যে পার্থক্য কী? (Difference between ArrayList and LinkedList in Java)
item.description: Lambda expressions are all about how we write code. It’s about how we can write more concise, smaller, and less boilerplate code. However, this aforementioned statement may not seem to be true in the case of exceptions in Java. In Java, we can only handle exceptions through the try-catch block, and this hasn’t changed for the lambda expression. Let’s say we’re going to develop a simple web crawler. The crawler will take a list of URLs in a string as an argument and save the content of the text file in a text file.
